text:
holy poop... I actually watched the whole thing without taking a single break.. even had to recap some of the funniest stuff to me like 3 different times..





I'm surprised it took 9 minutes into the "intro" section to mention the red eyes lol, but also not surprised at all since after I clicked on it I realized it was a collab.. and as for what you could put yourself as in the credits.. why not chief editor or lead producer or something like that? if it was really all just you doing mastering work in different pieces of software for the final vid, I'd see that as a fitting title! One more thing CS.. if by some chance you do see. Ya'know how when Linus Tech Tips isn't building someone a home theater or editing computer, he's working on a copy of at least an earlier rack-mount data storage solution similar to their first boxes? In my mind.. video sharing, if we all tried really hard, could break free from the internet entirely...

Think about this: Sharing content on the soon-to-go-unused blu-ray disc format. Those things if I am remembering right hold a minimum of 25GB of data on single layer, and up to 100GB with four! I know how to use youtube-dl, and have been practicing lately lol.. and.. BRUH!! what the!?! this video is at 1080p 60fps and over an hour long... and only 1.2GB of video?? Either YT compressed the file a tonnnn or you did.. or everyone's 'too-quick-for-youtube' edits got compressed too.. But what I'm trying to say is I wish on a fish that people would build the linus servers in their homes to master YTP collab compilations onto BDXL.. potentially all from video from disc as well.. "Lossless Video" from any source editors with the CPU / GPU power (just not a camera) can try to edit.. I had an idea not too long ago that involved a brief clip from a 1080p movie.. and even my newly upgraded PC still lagggs in blender, and at small preview too :(

I just finished the video, and the transition from "the youtube hell section says" into my "welcome to hell" clip, and directly into Gore king's insane clip hit me like a 2 ton slab of solid Lead. I had to rewatch the intro 5000 times during editing, so I got pretty comfortable with expecting the line after the gasp, but I don't think there was much preparing me for that. Amazing! 

The way you used my entry was more than I ever could've asked for. You have a skill for developing context within entropy. Seeing my clips sync in contextual harmony with some of the best YTPers I've seen is just such an unreal feeling. Everyone did such a fantastic job. Thank you again cs188.
